notes: Relay is a behavioral framework skill. Test cases are prompts; 'good' is described per case and broken into checkable assertions. Because this project's only spawning primitive is spawn_session (fire-and-forget, real sessions), the standard isolated-subagent benchmark pipeline is not available here. Verify via (a) inline behavioral walkthrough of the skill text and (b) live spawn_session smoke tests against a throwaway sandbox relay, observed by the human in the UI. Assertions tagged "script" can be checked by counting spawn_session calls / inspecting files; assertions tagged "judgment" need a human or grader read.

evals.name: plan-a-relay
evals.prompt: I want to migrate all our REST endpoints to the new validation layer — there are around 40 of them across src/server/routes. I won't be able to babysit this. Set it up as a relay so an agent can grind through it across sessions and only pull me in when it actually needs me.
evals.expected_output: Produces a charter (default .pi/relays/<name>/charter.md) plus an empty/seeded log. The charter has all four required slots present: goal/finish-line, sizing, handover, intervention signal. The agent ASKS the user to make sizing and the intervention signal concrete rather than inventing strict rules. It does NOT prescribe what a 'good' leg size or cadence is. It may dispatch the first leg only after the charter is agreed.

assertions.name: charter-created
assertions.text: A charter document is created (default under .pi/relays/<name>/ unless the user specified a location).

assertions.name: goal-slot-present
assertions.text: The charter defines a concrete, achievable finish line / goal.

assertions.name: sizing-slot-present
assertions.text: The charter states how much work is one leg (sizing), rather than leaving it undefined.

assertions.name: handover-slot-present
assertions.text: The charter states the handover mechanism (what the spawn prompt says and what the next runner reads).

assertions.name: intervention-slot-present
assertions.text: The charter defines an intervention signal: when/how a runner stops and gets the human.

assertions.name: asks-not-prescribes
assertions.text: For sizing and the intervention signal, the agent asks the user to make them concrete instead of imposing its own strict rules/cadence.

assertions.name: no-premature-spawn
assertions.text: The agent does not spawn the first leg before the charter is agreed with the user.

evals.name: run-one-leg-and-hand-off
evals.prompt: You're working under the Relay framework. Read .pi/relays/<sandbox>/charter.md and .pi/relays/<sandbox>/log.md, continue the plan, then dispatch the next agent.
evals.expected_output: Loads the relay skill (handoff prompt names the framework). Orients by reading charter+log, re-anchors to the goal, does exactly ONE well-sized leg per the charter's sizing, appends a log entry (what/why/new state/blockers), makes work durable (saves files, commits if the charter calls for it), then calls spawn_session exactly once with a handoff prompt that names Relay and points at the charter+log. Does not do extra legs or spawn more than once.

assertions.name: skill-loads-from-handoff
assertions.text: The agent recognizes it is in a relay and loads/consults the relay skill from the handoff prompt.

assertions.name: reads-charter-and-log
assertions.text: The agent reads both the charter and the log before acting.

assertions.name: exactly-one-leg
assertions.text: The agent completes exactly one well-sized leg, not several.

assertions.name: log-appended
assertions.text: A new log entry is appended recording what was done, decisions, new state, and any blocker.

assertions.name: work-durable-before-handoff
assertions.text: Work is saved (and committed if the charter requires it) before spawn_session is called.

assertions.name: spawn-exactly-once
assertions.text: spawn_session is called exactly once.

assertions.name: handoff-names-relay
assertions.text: The spawn prompt names the Relay framework and points the next runner at the charter and log so the skill loads downstream.

evals.name: stop-on-blocker-do-not-spawn
evals.prompt: You're working under the Relay framework. Read .pi/relays/<sandbox>/charter.md and .pi/relays/<sandbox>/log.md and continue. (The next leg hits a condition the charter flagged as requiring the human.)
evals.expected_output: Orients, begins the leg, recognizes the charter's intervention condition has fired. Stops cleanly: logs the blocker clearly, raises the charter's defined intervention signal so the watching human sees it, and does NOT call spawn_session. A clean stop with a clear blocker is the success condition here.

assertions.name: blocker-logged
assertions.text: The agent logs the blocker clearly in the log.

assertions.name: intervention-signal-raised
assertions.text: The agent raises the charter's defined intervention signal so the human can see it.

assertions.name: does-not-spawn
assertions.text: spawn_session is NOT called when blocked.

assertions.name: no-silent-stall
assertions.text: The agent does not stop silently; the stop is explained and visible.

evals.name: negative-no-magic-load
evals.prompt: Plan a multi-step refactor of our auth module and then spawn a session to start working on it. Break it into stages.
evals.expected_output: This prompt mentions a multi-step plan AND spawning a session, but never names the Relay framework, points at a charter/log, or invokes the skill. The relay skill should NOT load. The agent should plan and (optionally) use spawn_session as ordinary tools, without adopting relay ceremony (charter/log/legs/intervention signal).

assertions.name: skill-does-not-load
assertions.text: The relay skill does NOT trigger for this prompt.

assertions.name: no-relay-ceremony
assertions.text: The agent does not create a charter/log or impose relay leg/handoff ceremony.
